text.title: php-eloquent Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for php-eloquent as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Model::all() on a user-facing endpoint without pagination.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Loads the entire table into PHP memory. OOM risk on tables with >10k rows.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: All columns become mass-assignable, allowing an attacker to set is_admin, organization_id, or any sensitive field via the API.

failure-mode.description: Mixing old getEmailAttribute() accessor and new Attribute::make() for the same attribute on the same model.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Produces undefined behavior; old-style accessors silently win in some Laravel versions, causing the new accessor to be ignored.
